concerns.text: FR-004 explicitly mandates '5-fold' cross-validation. T019 describes a 'stratified data splitter' but omits the specific '5-fold' count, leaving the implementation open to generic K-fold which fails the spec requirement.

FR-003 requires the system to verify 'torch.cuda.is_available() is False' and log 'Device: CPU' at the 'startup' of the pipeline. T005b creates a utility file but does not explicitly task the integration of this check into the main training entry point, risking the check being defined but never executed at startup.

FR-006 requires an ablation study that reports the 'variation in correlation coefficients' by removing solvent descriptors. T025 tasks the removal of descriptors to 'isolate graph contribution' but fails to explicitly mandate the generation of the comparative report showing the variation, which is the core success criterion.

SC-003 requires measuring runtime against the 'free-tier CI runner time limit' (6 hours). T006 implements a 'fixed runtime limit' but does not specify the 6-hour threshold or link it to the CI constraint, leaving the success criterion unverified.

The spec's Edge Case requires sampling/truncation for high molecular weight molecules to prevent memory crashes. T006b implements 'sampling or truncation logic' but does not specify that this must occur 'during featurization' (before memory allocation), risking the crash if applied too late in the pipeline.

FR-004 mandates stratification 'by solvent type'. T019 mentions a 'stratified data splitter' but omits the specific 'by solvent type' constraint, creating a risk that an implementer defaults to standard label stratification.

T018 implements a 'Solvent-Only' baseline. While useful, FR-005 and SC-002 strictly define the primary statistical test as GNN vs. Linear Regression (with descriptors). T018 is not explicitly tagged to a specific FR/SC in the spec, creating a traceability gap for this extra scope.

T007 relies on a hardcoded fallback URL 'zenodo.org/records/1234567...' which appears to be a placeholder. The Constitution (Principle II) requires verified citations. This task lacks a verified dataset citation, risking a violation of the 'Verified Accuracy' principle.
